Medical images have traditionally been m: Medical images have traditionally been manually segmented, which is a time-consuming process that requires intensive effort by radiologists and is subject to inter-reader variability.
In the past decade, image segmentation h:
In the past decade, image segmentation has been powered by the development of deep learning algorithms (e.g. nnUnet by the German Cancer Research Center (DKFZ)/Helmholtz Research).


AI-powered segmentation tools can reduce the segmentation time and provide more reproducible results.
A Model is an AI algorithm that was trai:
A Model is an AI algorithm that was trained to perform a specific task (e.g. brain tumor segmentation model).

The Weights of an AI model are small numbers that determine how much importance the model gives to different image features.

During the Training phase, a model learns patterns from data labelled by experts and adjusts its weights to improve its predictions.

During the Validation/Test phase, the model is evaluated on a separate set of data not used during the Training phase.

During Inference, the model is applied to new datasets to perform the specific task it was trained for.
This tutorial focuses on running inferen: This tutorial focuses on running inference tasks using various pre-trained AI models for automated segmentation of anatomical and pathological structures.
This tutorial uses the pre-trained model:
This tutorial uses the pre-trained models of the MONAIAuto3DSeg Slicer extension.


The tool is designed to work on laptops or on average desktop computer without a GPU.
Multiple modalities Support (CT, MRI).\n:
Multiple modalities Support (CT, MRI).


Multiple anatomies (head, thorax, abdomen, pelvis, etc.).


Multiple pathologies (tumor, hemorrhage, edema).

Select the MONAIAuto3DSeg module

AI-based Segmentation of Neoplasm, Necro:
AI-based Segmentation of Neoplasm, Necrosis and Edema in Brain MRI images.


Datasets:
1) BraTS-GLI_00005-000-t1n (T1-weighted)
2) BraTS-GLI_00005-000-t1c (T1-weighted post-Gd)
3) BraTS-GLI_00005-000-t2w (T2-weighted)
4) BraTS-GLI_00005-000-t2f (T2-FLAIR )
Click on Add Data in the Welcome to Slic:
Click on Add Data in the Welcome to Slicer module

Click on Choose File(s) to Add and browse to the location of the Slicer datasets

In the subdirectory dataset4_BrainMRI_Glioma, select the four datasets BraTS-GLI-00006-t1c.nii.gz, BraTS-GLI-00006-t1n.nii.gz, BraTS-GLI-00006-t2f.nii.gz, BraTS-GLI-00006-t2w.nii.gz

Click on Open
Select the module MONAIAuto3DSeg and ent: Select the module MONAIAuto3DSeg and enter the model's name Brain Tumor Segmentation in the Segmentation model menu
Select the model Brain Tumor Segmentatio: Select the model Brain Tumor Segmentation (BRATS) GLI
Enter the input volumes as follows:\n\nI:
Enter the input volumes as follows:

Input T2F volume: BraTS-GLI_00005-000-t2f
Input T1C volume: BraTS-GLI_00005-000-t1c
Input T1N volume: BraTS-GLI_00005-000-t1n
Input T2W volume: BraTS-GLI_00005-000-t2w


Click on Create new Segmentation on Apply

Click on Apply to start the segmentation
Slicer starts running the inference task:
Slicer starts running the inference task

Once the segmentation is done, 'Processing finished' appears in the Slicer GUI
Click on Show 3D to display the 3D segme: Click on Show 3D to display the 3D segments in the 3D Viewer

The 3D Slicer MONAIAuto3DSeg extension p:
The 3D Slicer MONAIAuto3DSeg extension provides fast AI-based segmentation of anatomical and pathological structures.


The module can run on standard laptop and desktop computers with no GPU.
